verified.name: Verome
verified.tagline: Search music, charts, lyrics and radio via YouTube Music
verified.description: Verome is a keyless Deno-hosted API that bundles music search, charts, trending tracks, synced lyrics and radio mixes sourced from YouTube Music, YouTube and Last.fm. Hit /api/search with a query and it returns structured artist, song and album results as JSON. Because it proxies third-party services, treat uptime and data rights as those platforms' own.
verified.notes: Community instance on Deno Deploy; proxies YouTube Music / YouTube / Last.fm data.

verified.name: Archive.org
verified.tagline: Metadata and file listings for Internet Archive items
verified.description: The Internet Archive's metadata endpoint returns everything it knows about an item: its files, formats, checksums, servers and descriptive fields, all as JSON. Just append an item identifier to /metadata/ — no key, no signup. Handy for building on top of the Archive's books, audio, video and web collections.

verified.name: BotsArchive
verified.tagline: Look up Telegram bots by username in a curated catalog
verified.description: BotsArchive maintains a hand-curated database of Telegram bots and exposes their details through a small PHP API. Query /getBotID.php with a bot's username and it returns the bot's ID, category, tags, star rating and description as JSON. No key is required for the public lookups.
verified.notes: API host is api.botsarchive.com (the docs domain's /api.php returns 404).

verified.name: Callook.info
verified.tagline: Look up US ham radio callsigns and licensee details
verified.description: Callook turns any United States amateur-radio callsign into structured JSON: license class, trustee, mailing address, grid square and lat/long. Append /json to a callsign and you're done — no key needed. The data traces back to the FCC's public ULS licensing records.
verified.dataLicense: Unverified (data from public FCC ULS records)

verified.name: French Address Search
verified.tagline: Geocode French addresses via the government's BAN
verified.description: France's official Base Adresse Nationale search endpoint geocodes a free-text address into GeoJSON, complete with coordinates, a match score and normalized address parts. Pass ?q= with any street and it answers instantly, keyless. It's the same open service that underpins many French public-sector maps.

verified.name: Lowy Asia Power Index
verified.tagline: Rankings of state power across Asia, as JSON
verified.description: The Lowy Institute's Asia Power Index scores and ranks countries on their comprehensive power across the region, and these endpoints surface the underlying data as JSON. Fetch /data/{year}.json (2018 onward) for per-country scores, ranks and year-on-year trends. The data is the Lowy Institute's own research, exposed here through a community-written API guide.
verified.notes: Fields are terse: c = ISO country code. Docs list /countries.json but it now 404s; /data/{year}.json and /world.json work.

verified.name: Onyx Bazaar
verified.tagline: Leaderboard of paid x402 HTTP services on-chain
verified.description: Onyx Bazaar is a public leaderboard of x402 pay-per-call HTTP services, indexed from Coinbase's CDP discovery API and refreshed roughly every 15 minutes. The /bazaar.json endpoint returns ranked rows carrying each service's URL, price, network and 30-day call and payer counts. Keyless, CORS-friendly JSON.
verified.notes: The /bazaar page is HTML; the JSON lives at /bazaar.json.

verified.name: Universities List
verified.tagline: Search universities by name, country and domain
verified.description: This community dataset (Hipolabs) lets you search universities worldwide and get back their names, countries, web pages and email domains as JSON. Filter with ?name= and/or ?country= — no key, no signup. Note the public host is HTTP-only; there is no working HTTPS endpoint.
verified.notes: HTTP-only — port 443 refuses connections, so no HTTPS despite the listing.

verified.name: University of Oslo
verified.tagline: Open course and study-programme data from UiO
verified.description: The University of Oslo publishes its study information as open JSON APIs — courses, semesters, schedules, exams and programmes. Ask /studies/v1/course/{code} for a course's fundamentals, all keyless. Descriptive fields often come back in Norwegian.
verified.notes: Also exposes /org/v1 and /syllabus/v1; the /studies/v1/ index itself is HTML documentation.

verified.name: Voidly
verified.description: Voidly aggregates network measurements into a live picture of internet censorship: which domains are blocked, where, and how deeply. The keyless /v1/measurement/summary gives a one-call global snapshot, while /data/censorship-index.json ships the full ranked dataset with Schema.org markup. The index is published under CC BY 4.0, so credit Voidly if you use it.
verified.freeTier: Free (keyless data endpoints; Hydra ML endpoints need a key)
verified.notes: License and attribution request come from the dataset's own license field; listing said cors:no but responses send access-control-allow-origin: *.

verified.name: Wikipedia
verified.tagline: Query Wikipedia articles through the MediaWiki API
verified.description: The MediaWiki Action API is the machine door into Wikipedia: query page metadata, content, links and search as JSON. This sample uses action=query for a page's basic info — no key required, though Wikimedia asks you to send a descriptive User-Agent and be gentle. Article content is licensed CC BY-SA.
verified.rateLimit: Unpublished (follow Wikimedia etiquette; send a descriptive User-Agent)
verified.notes: CORS is enabled per-request via an origin param; the plain call sends no access-control-allow-origin header.

verified.name: Datamuse
verified.tagline: A word-finding engine for rhymes, synonyms and more
verified.description: Datamuse answers word queries: give it constraints like 'rhymes with blue' or 'means almost the same as X' and it returns ranked word suggestions as JSON. The /words endpoint takes parameters such as rel_rhy, ml and sp, all keyless. Great for writing tools, word games and autocomplete.
verified.notes: CORS header echoes the request Origin rather than sending '*'.

verified.name: Drupal.org
verified.tagline: Read Drupal.org projects, issues and users as JSON
verified.description: Drupal.org exposes its own content through a read-only Services API, returning nodes — issues, projects, users and more — as paginated JSON. Hit /api-d7/node.json to page through the data; no key is needed for reads. Useful for dashboards over the Drupal ecosystem.

verified.name: Shields
verified.tagline: Badge data (and SVG), now available as JSON too
verified.description: Shields.io is the badge factory behind millions of READMEs. Beyond the usual SVG, appending .json to a /badge/ path returns the badge's label, message and color as structured JSON. It's free and keyless; very heavy users are asked to self-host.
verified.notes: Primarily an SVG/raster badge service; the .json suffix returns the badge fields as JSON.

verified.name: Advice Slip
verified.tagline: Random one-line life advice, served as JSON
verified.description: The Advice Slip API dishes out random pieces of advice, one slip at a time. Call /advice and you get a JSON object with an id and a single line of (often tongue-in-cheek) wisdom. No key, no signup, just advice.

slip.advice: Don't ever name files or folders using the word "Final".

verified.name: icanhazdadjoke
verified.tagline: The internet's biggest pile of dad jokes, as JSON
verified.description: icanhazdadjoke serves random dad jokes from a large collection. Send an Accept: application/json header to the root URL and it returns a joke plus its id as JSON (otherwise you get plain text or HTML). Keyless and delightfully groan-worthy.

sampleJson.joke: Why does Waldo only wear stripes? Because he doesn't want to be spotted.

verified.notes: Requires an 'Accept: application/json' header — the default response is text/HTML.

skipped.name: Inshorts News
skipped.reason: Hosted endpoint (inshorts.deta.dev) no longer resolves and the old inshortsapi.vercel.app returns 402 deployment-disabled; only a self-hostable Python scraper remains, no live keyless JSON GET.

skipped.name: CollegeScoreCard.ed.gov
skipped.reason: Requires an api.data.gov API key; keyless request returns HTTP 403 API_KEY_MISSING.

skipped.name: Open Data Minneapolis
skipped.reason: Only site-wide keyless JSON endpoint is the full DCAT-US catalog feed (multi-MB); it exceeds the 8s probe with no small representative GET available without per-dataset ArcGIS service URLs.

skipped.name: PatentsView
skipped.reason: Legacy query API retired; api.patentsview.org now serves the Open Data Portal SPA (HTML) and the replacement Search API requires an API key.

skipped.name: USPTO
skipped.reason: Data APIs migrated to the keyed Open Data Portal; developer.uspto.gov endpoints return the portal SPA/HTML or 503 and assignment-api.uspto.gov no longer resolves — no keyless JSON GET found.

skipped.name: Dictum
skipped.reason: Project migrated to quoterism.com, whose API now requires an X-API-Key header; no keyless GET remains.

skipped.name: Programming Quotes
skipped.reason: Hosted service programming-quotes-api.azurewebsites.net no longer resolves (NXDOMAIN); the API is dead.

skipped.name: Quotable Quotes
skipped.reason: api.quotable.io no longer resolves (DNS NXDOMAIN); the hosted service has shut down.
